facts.context: n 1931, Barbara McClintock discovered that genes could change position on a chromosome. In corn plants, if a certain gene was moved next to the gene that controls kernel color, the kernels in an ear of corn were various colors. Which statement best supports McClintock's discovery?
facts.truth: Genes control inherited traits in organisms.
distractors: Corn plants can reproduce in laboratory environments.
Chromosomes can move around in the nucleus of a cell.
Genes are able to disappear and reappear in later generations.

facts.context: All living things are made up of prokaryotic or eukaryotic cells. Which function is found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
A species of wasp lays its eggs in the larva of another insect. The wasp is a parasite, and the larva is the host. What is the relationship between the parasite and the host?
facts.truth: The parasite benefits and the host is harmed.
distractors: Both the parasite and the host benefit.
Both the parasite and the host are harmed.
The parasite is harmed and the host benefits.

facts.context: The structures in the bodies of many organisms have levels of organization that help the body to carry out life processes. Which list correctly shows how these levels are organized from the simplest to the most complex?

distractors: the formation of new proteins for the cell
the removal of waste products from the cell

A euglena can live in fresh water.
A euglena can regulate water intake.

facts.context: Finding fossils of warm-weather plants in the rock layers of a cold northern island suggests that
facts.truth: the climate of the island used to be warmer.

distractors: To fight disease in the body

facts.context: Which best describes how earthworms improve soil as they eat dead plant material?
facts.truth: They recycle nutrients into the soil.
distractors: They keep disease out of the soil.

facts.context: Use the information below to answer the question. Cotton is a plant product used to make fabric. Cotton is made of cellulose, a fiber not digestible by humans. How would a clothing manufacturer separate colors to determine the purity of the dyes?
What property of water is most important for living organisms?
facts.truth: It is liquid at most temperatures on Earth.

distractors: sea level to a valley below sea level.
a beach at sea level to a valley at sea level.

facts.truth: The plants will compete with algae for nutrients.
distractors: The plants make the pond look beautiful.

She placed one group on a windowsill in the sun and the other group in a dark closet on the cool (north) side of the house. facts.truth: The amount of light was different for the two groups.
distractors: The temperature difference between the two groups was not great enough.

Nothing is wrong with the experiment.

facts.truth: The remains were carried by ocean currents.
distractors: A great land bridge once connected the continents.
